verses.greek: Παῦλος δοῦλος ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃, κλητὸς ἀπόστολος, ἀφωρισμένος εἰς εὐαγγέλιον θεοῦ

verses.mistral: Through whom we have received grace and apostleship to offer obedience of faith among all the Gentiles for His name.
verses.kjv: By whom we have received grace and apostleship, for obedience to the faith among all nations, for his name:

verses.greek: ἐν οἷς ἐστε καὶ ὑμεῖς κλητοὶ Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ,
verses.mistral: You are called in Christ Jesus
verses.kjv: Among whom are ye also the called of Jesus Christ:

verses.mistral: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: To all that be in Rome, beloved of God, called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.greek: Πρῶτον μὲν εὐχαριστῶ τῷ θεῷ μου διὰ Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ ⸀περὶ πάντων ὑμῶν, ὅτι ἡ πίστις ὑμῶν καταγγέλλεται ἐν ὅλῳ τῷ κόσμῳ.
verses.mistral: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith is being proclaimed throughout the whole world.
verses.kjv: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for you all, that your faith is spoken of throughout the whole world.

verses.greek: μάρτυς γάρ μού ἐστιν ὁ θεός, ᾧ λατρεύω ἐν τῷ πνεύματί μου ἐν τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ τοῦ υἱοῦ αὐτοῦ, ὡς ἀδιαλείπτως μνείαν ὑμῶν ποιοῦμαι
verses.mistral: My witness God is, to whom I serve in my spirit in the gospel of His Son, as unfailingly I make a remembrance of you.
verses.kjv: For God is my witness, whom I serve with my spirit in the gospel of his Son, that without ceasing I make mention of you always in my prayers;

verses.greek: πάντοτε ἐπὶ τῶν προσευχῶν μου, δεόμενος εἴ πως ἤδη ποτὲ εὐοδωθήσομαι ἐν τῷ θελήματι τοῦ θεοῦ ἐλθεῖν πρὸς ὑμᾶς.
verses.mistral: Always on my prayers, I am asking if in any way I might already have been made worthy to come to you according to God's will.
verses.kjv: Making request, if by any means now at length I might have a prosperous journey by the will of God to come unto you.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.mistral: I long to see you, so that I may impart a spiritual gift to you to establish you
verses.kjv: For I long to see you, that I may impart unto you some spiritual gift, to the end ye may be established;

words.gloss: I long for, strain after, desire greatly

verses.mistral: This is being linked together in you by our mutual faith, both yours and mine.

verses.mistral: I have not wanted, brothers, that you be ignorant, that I have often been intending to come to you, and have been hindered until now, so that I might reap some fruit among you also, even as among the other Gentiles.
verses.kjv: Now I would not have you ignorant, brethren, that oftentimes I purposed to come unto you, (but was let hitherto,) that I might have some fruit among you also, even as among other Gentiles.

I do not know, am ignorant of

verses.kjv: I am debtor both to the Greeks, and to the Barbarians; both to the wise, and to the unwise.

verses.greek: οὕτως τὸ κατ’ ἐμὲ πρόθυμον καὶ ὑμῖν τοῖς ἐν Ῥώμῃ εὐαγγελίσασθαι.
verses.mistral: Thus it is my eager desire and necessity to preach the gospel to you who are in Rome.
verses.kjv: So, as much as in me is, I am ready to preach the gospel to you that are at Rome also.

verses.mistral: I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
verses.kjv: For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ: for it is the power of God unto salvation to every one that believeth; to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.

verses.greek: δικαιοσύνη γὰρ θεοῦ ἐν αὐτῷ ἀποκαλύπτεται ἐκ πίστεως εἰς πίστιν, καθὼς γέγραπται· Ὁ δὲ δίκαιος ἐκ πίστεως ζήσεται.
verses.mistral: Righteousness of God is revealed in it (the gospel) from faith to faith, as it is written: The one who is justified lives by faith.
verses.kjv: For therein is the righteousness of God revealed from faith to faith: as it is written, The just shall live by faith.

verses.mistral: Wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all unrighteousness and wickedness of men who suppress the truth in wickedness.
verses.kjv: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who hold the truth in unrighteousness;

verses.kjv: Because that which may be known of God is manifest in them; for God hath shewed it unto them.

verses.greek: τὰ γὰρ ἀόρατα αὐτοῦ ἀπὸ κτίσεως κόσμου τοῖς ποιήμασιν νοούμενα καθορᾶται, ἥ τε ἀΐδιος αὐτοῦ δύναμις καὶ θειότης, εἰς τὸ εἶναι αὐτοὺς ἀναπολογήτους,
verses.mistral: The unseen things of Him from creation are clearly perceived, and the divine nature and deity, for their being declared as being justified.
verses.kjv: For the invisible things of him from the creation of the world are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made, even his eternal power and Godhead; so that they are without excuse:

verses.greek: διότι γνόντες τὸν θεὸν οὐχ ὡς θεὸν ἐδόξασαν ἢ ηὐχαρίστησαν, ἀλλὰ ἐματαιώθησαν ἐν τοῖς διαλογισμοῖς αὐτῶν καὶ ἐσκοτίσθη ἡ ἀσύνετος αὐτῶν καρδία·
verses.mistral: They did not glorify or thank Him as God, but became futile in their thinking and their senseless minds were darkened.
verses.kjv: Because that, when they knew God, they glorified him not as God, neither were thankful; but became vain in their imaginations, and their foolish heart was darkened.

verses.kjv: For this cause God gave them up unto vile affections: for even their women did change the natural use into that which is against nature:

verses.mistral: Men, in the same way, abandoned natural intercourse with women and were inflamed in their lust for one another. Men committing indecent acts with other men, and received within themselves the due penalty for their perversion.
verses.kjv: And likewise also the men, leaving the natural use of the woman, burned in their lust one toward another; men with men working that which is unseemly, and receiving in themselves that recompence of their error which was meet.

words.gloss: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.mistral: God gave them over to an unreasonable mind, to do what is not right.
verses.kjv: And even as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge, God gave them over to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not convenient;

words.gloss: I put to the test, prove, examine

verses.kjv: Being filled with all unrighteousness, fornication, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, debate, deceit, malignity; whisperers,

verses.kjv: Backbiters, haters of God, despiteful, proud, boasters, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,

verses.mistral: Unintelligent, unrighteous, godless, merciless
verses.kjv: Without understanding, covenantbreakers, without natural affection, implacable, unmerciful:

verses.mistral: Understanding that the righteousness of God is evident, that those who do such things are worthy of death—not only doing them but also approving of those who do—
verses.kjv: Who knowing the judgment of God, that they which commit such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but have pleasure in them that do them.

I consent, agree, am of one mind with

verses.kjv: Therefore thou art inexcusable, O man, whosoever thou art that judgest: for wherein thou judgest another, thou condemnest thyself; for thou that judgest doest the same things.

verses.greek: οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι τὸ κρίμα τοῦ θεοῦ ἐστιν κατὰ ἀλήθειαν ἐπὶ τοὺς τὰ τοιαῦτα πράσσοντας.
verses.mistral: The judgment of God is according to truth on those who practice such things.
verses.kjv: But we are sure that the judgment of God is according to truth against them which commit such things.

verses.mistral: You are contemplating this, O man, who judges those practicing such things and doing them, that you will escape the judgment of God?
verses.kjv: And thinkest thou this, O man, that judgest them which do such things, and doest the same, that thou shalt escape the judgment of God?

verses.kjv: Or despisest thou the riches of his goodness and forbearance and longsuffering; not knowing that the goodness of God leadeth thee to repentance?

verses.kjv: Who will render to every man according to his deeds:

verses.mistral: Seek for themselves glory, honor, and imperishable life through endurance in good works.
verses.kjv: To them who by patient continuance in well doing seek for glory and honour and immortality, eternal life:

verses.mistral: Affliction and hardship befall every human who does evil, first to the Jew, then to the Greek.
verses.kjv: Tribulation and anguish, upon every soul of man that doeth evil, of the Jew first, and also of the Gentile;

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: δόξα δὲ καὶ τιμὴ καὶ εἰρήνη παντὶ τῷ ἐργαζομένῳ τὸ ἀγαθόν, Ἰουδαίῳ τε πρῶτον καὶ Ἕλληνι·
verses.mistral: Glory and honor and peace to everyone who does good, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
verses.kjv: But glory, honour, and peace, to every man that worketh good, to the Jew first, and also to the Gentile:

verses.kjv: (For not the hearers of the law are just before God, but the doers of the law shall be justified.

verses.mistral: Natural law is written in their hearts, to them the law is. (This verse is Romans 2:15 in the King James Version of the Bible)
verses.kjv: For when the Gentiles, which have not the law, do by nature the things contained in the law, these, having not the law, are a law unto themselves:

verses.mistral: In the day when God judges the hidden things of men according to my gospel through Christ Jesus.

verses.mistral: If, however, you are a Jew, and rest on the law, and boast in God

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize
I put to the test, prove, examine
I carry through, differ, surpass, excel

verses.greek: πέποιθάς τε σεαυτὸν ὁδηγὸν εἶναι τυφλῶν, φῶς τῶν ἐν σκότει,
verses.mistral: Lead blinded ones, you have become a guide; light in the midst of darkness.
verses.kjv: And art confident that thou thyself art a guide of the blind, a light of them which are in darkness,

verses.greek: παιδευτὴν ἀφρόνων, διδάσκαλον νηπίων, ἔχοντα τὴν μόρφωσιν τῆς γνώσεως καὶ τῆς ἀληθείας ἐν τῷ νόμῳ—
verses.mistral: Educator of the foolish, teacher of infants, possessing the form of knowledge and truth in the law.
verses.kjv: An instructor of the foolish, a teacher of babes, which hast the form of knowledge and of the truth in the law.

verses.greek: ὁ οὖν διδάσκων ἕτερον σεαυτὸν οὐ διδάσκεις; ὁ κηρύσσων μὴ κλέπτειν κλέπτεις;
verses.mistral: You are teaching one thing, but you practice another. You preach not to steal, yet you steal.
verses.kjv: Thou therefore which teachest another, teachest thou not thyself? thou that preachest a man should not steal, dost thou steal?

verses.greek: ὁ λέγων μὴ μοιχεύειν μοιχεύεις; ὁ βδελυσσόμενος τὰ εἴδωλα ἱεροσυλεῖς;
verses.mistral: You who say not to commit adultery, do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples?
verses.kjv: Thou that sayest a man should not commit adultery, dost thou commit adultery? thou that abhorrest idols, dost thou commit sacrilege?

verses.greek: ὃς ἐν νόμῳ καυχᾶσαι, διὰ τῆς παραβάσεως τοῦ νόμου τὸν θεὸν ἀτιμάζεις;
verses.mistral: You who boast in the law dishonor God through your disregard for the law.
verses.kjv: Thou that makest thy boast of the law, through breaking the law dishonourest thou God?

verses.greek: τὸ γὰρ ὄνομα τοῦ θεοῦ δι’ ὑμᾶς βλασφημεῖται ἐν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν, καθὼς γέγραπται.
verses.mistral: The name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you.
verses.kjv: For the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles through you, as it is written.

verses.greek: Περιτομὴ μὲν γὰρ ὠφελεῖ ἐὰν νόμον πράσσῃς· ἐὰν δὲ παραβάτης νόμου ᾖς, ἡ περιτομή σου ἀκροβυστία γέγονεν.
verses.mistral: Circumcision indeed is profitable if you keep the law; but if you are a transgressor of the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.
verses.kjv: For circumcision verily profiteth, if thou keep the law: but if thou be a breaker of the law, thy circumcision is made uncircumcision.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ἐὰν οὖν ἡ ἀκροβυστία τὰ δικαιώματα τοῦ νόμου φυλάσσῃ, ⸀οὐχ ἡ ἀκροβυστία αὐτοῦ εἰς περιτομὴν λογισθήσεται;
verses.mistral: If circumcision serves the law, will it be reckoned as a transgression?
verses.kjv: Therefore if the uncircumcision keep the righteousness of the law, shall not his uncircumcision be counted for circumcision?

verses.greek: καὶ κρινεῖ ἡ ἐκ φύσεως ἀκροβυστία τὸν νόμον τελοῦσα σὲ τὸν διὰ γράμματος καὶ περιτομῆς παραβάτην νόμου.
verses.mistral: Condemns the uncircumcision, which fulfills the law, you by writings and circumcision, transgressor of the law.
verses.kjv: And shall not uncircumcision which is by nature, if it fulfil the law, judge thee, who by the letter and circumcision dost transgress the law?

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ ὁ ἐν τῷ φανερῷ Ἰουδαῖός ἐστιν, οὐδὲ ἡ ἐν τῷ φανερῷ ἐν σαρκὶ περιτομή·
verses.mistral: Not a Jew inwardly, nor circumcision inwardly.
verses.kjv: For he is not a Jew, which is one outwardly; neither is that circumcision, which is outward in the flesh:

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ ὁ ἐν τῷ κρυπτῷ Ἰουδαῖος, καὶ περιτομὴ καρδίας ἐν πνεύματι οὐ γράμματι, οὗ ὁ ἔπαινος οὐκ ἐξ ἀνθρώπων ἀλλ’ ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: Hidden Jew, and circumcision of the heart by spirit, not by letter, whose praise is not from man but from God.
verses.kjv: But he is a Jew, which is one inwardly; and circumcision is that of the heart, in the spirit, and not in the letter; whose praise is not of men, but of God.

verses.greek: Τί οὖν τὸ περισσὸν τοῦ Ἰουδαίου, ἢ τίς ἡ ὠφέλεια τῆς περιτομῆς;
verses.mistral: What is the advantage then of the Jew, or what is the benefit of circumcision?
verses.kjv: What advantage then hath the Jew? or what profit is there of circumcision?

verses.greek: πολὺ κατὰ πάντα τρόπον. πρῶτον μὲν ⸀γὰρ ὅτι ἐπιστεύθησαν τὰ λόγια τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: Much in every way. First, because they believed the words of God.
verses.kjv: Much every way: chiefly, because that unto them were committed the oracles of God.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: τί γάρ; εἰ ἠπίστησάν τινες, μὴ ἡ ἀπιστία αὐτῶν τὴν πίστιν τοῦ θεοῦ καταργήσει;
verses.mistral: If anyone has denied, will their unbelief destroy the faith of God?
verses.kjv: For what if some did not believe? shall their unbelief make the faith of God without effect?

words.gloss: I bring to naught, sever, abolish

verses.greek: μὴ γένοιτο· γινέσθω δὲ ὁ θεὸς ἀληθής, πᾶς δὲ ἄνθρωπος ψεύστης, ⸀καθὼς γέγραπται· Ὅπως ἂν δικαιωθῇς ἐν τοῖς λόγοις σου καὶ ⸀νικήσεις ἐν τῷ κρίνεσθαί σε.
verses.mistral: Let God be true, but every man a liar, as it is written: "How can you say that which is right when you judge another?"
verses.kjv: God forbid: yea, let God be true, but every man a liar; as it is written, That thou mightest be justified in thy sayings, and mightest overcome when thou art judged.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
I come into being, am born
but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify

verses.greek: εἰ δὲ ἡ ἀδικία ἡμῶν θεοῦ δικαιοσύνην συνίστησιν, τί ἐροῦμεν; μὴ ἄδικος ὁ θεὸς ὁ ἐπιφέρων τὴν ὀργήν; κατὰ ἄνθρωπον λέγω.
verses.mistral: If indeed our iniquity merits God's righteousness, what shall we say? Shall God be unjust to inflict wrath? According to man I speak.
verses.kjv: But if our unrighteousness commend the righteousness of God, what shall we say? Is God unrighteous who taketh vengeance? (I speak as a man)

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: μὴ γένοιτο· ἐπεὶ πῶς κρινεῖ ὁ θεὸς τὸν κόσμον;
verses.mistral: God forbid that God should judge the world.
verses.kjv: God forbid: for then how shall God judge the world?

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: εἰ ⸀δὲ ἡ ἀλήθεια τοῦ θεοῦ ἐν τῷ ἐμῷ ψεύσματι ἐπερίσσευσεν εἰς τὴν δόξαν αὐτοῦ, τί ἔτι κἀγὼ ὡς ἁμαρτωλὸς κρίνομαι,
verses.mistral: If the truth of God has increased through my unrighteousness to His glory, what am I still being judged as a sinner?
verses.kjv: For if the truth of God hath more abounded through my lie unto his glory; why yet am I also judged as a sinner?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ μὴ καθὼς βλασφημούμεθα καὶ καθώς φασίν τινες ἡμᾶς λέγειν ὅτι Ποιήσωμεν τὰ κακὰ ἵνα ἔλθῃ τὰ ἀγαθά; ὧν τὸ κρίμα ἔνδικόν ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: And yet not, as we are blasphemed and as some say that we say, Let us do evil that good may come? To whom such reproach is fitting.
verses.kjv: And not rather, (as we be slanderously reported, and as some affirm that we say,) Let us do evil, that good may come? whose damnation is just.

verses.greek: Τί οὖν; προεχόμεθα; οὐ πάντως, προῃτιασάμεθα γὰρ Ἰουδαίους τε καὶ Ἕλληνας πάντας ὑφ’ ἁμαρτίαν εἶναι,
verses.mistral: Therefore we maintain? Are we not rather inducing both Jews and Greeks to be under sin?
verses.kjv: What then? are we better than they? No, in no wise: for we have before proved both Jews and Gentiles, that they are all under sin;

verses.greek: καθὼς γέγραπται ὅτι Οὐκ ἔστιν δίκαιος οὐδὲ εἷς,
verses.mistral: There is none righteous, not even one.
verses.kjv: As it is written, There is none righteous, no, not one:

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔστιν ⸀ὁ συνίων, οὐκ ἔστιν ⸁ὁ ἐκζητῶν τὸν θεόν·
verses.mistral: There is no one understanding, there is no one seeking God.
verses.kjv: There is none that understandeth, there is none that seeketh after God.

verses.greek: πάντες ἐξέκλιναν, ἅμα ἠχρεώθησαν· οὐκ ⸀ἔστιν ποιῶν χρηστότητα, οὐκ ἔστιν ἕως ἑνός.
verses.mistral: All turned aside, they were exchanged; none is righteous, not even one.
verses.kjv: They are all gone out of the way, they are together become unprofitable; there is none that doeth good, no, not one.

words.gloss: I fall away from, turn away
at the same time, along with

verses.greek: τάφος ἀνεῳγμένος ὁ λάρυγξ αὐτῶν, ταῖς γλώσσαις αὐτῶν ἐδολιοῦσαν, ἰὸς ἀσπίδων ὑπὸ τὰ χείλη αὐτῶν,
verses.mistral: Open graves are their mouths, speaking deceitfully with their tongues.
verses.kjv: Their throat is an open sepulchre; with their tongues they have used deceit; the poison of asps is under their lips:

verses.greek: ὧν τὸ στόμα ἀρᾶς καὶ πικρίας γέμει·
verses.mistral: Their mouths are full of venom and bitter deceit
verses.kjv: Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness:

words.gloss: a particle asking a question, expecting a negative response

verses.greek: ὀξεῖς οἱ πόδες αὐτῶν ἐκχέαι αἷμα,
verses.mistral: Sharp are their feet in shedding blood
verses.kjv: Their feet are swift to shed blood:

verses.greek: σύντριμμα καὶ ταλαιπωρία ἐν ταῖς ὁδοῖς αὐτῶν,
verses.mistral: Endurance and patience in their ways.
verses.kjv: Destruction and misery are in their ways:

verses.greek: καὶ ὁδὸν εἰρήνης οὐκ ἔγνωσαν.
verses.mistral: Peaceful path they did not know.
verses.kjv: And the way of peace have they not known:

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔστιν φόβος θεοῦ ἀπέναντι τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: Fear of God is not for those before His eyes.
verses.kjv: There is no fear of God before their eyes.

words.gloss: over against, opposite, in view of, in the presence of

verses.greek: Οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι ὅσα ὁ νόμος λέγει τοῖς ἐν τῷ νόμῳ λαλεῖ, ἵνα πᾶν στόμα φραγῇ καὶ ὑπόδικος γένηται πᾶς ὁ κόσμος τῷ θεῷ·
verses.mistral: We know that whatever the law says, it speaks to those who are under the law, so that every mouth may be silenced and the whole world may become accountable to God.
verses.kjv: Now we know that what things soever the law saith, it saith to them who are under the law: that every mouth may be stopped, and all the world may become guilty before God.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: διότι ἐξ ἔργων νόμου οὐ δικαιωθήσεται πᾶσα σὰρξ ἐνώπιον αὐτοῦ, διὰ γὰρ νόμου ἐπίγνωσις ἁμαρτίας.
verses.mistral: Justified will not be every flesh by the works of the law, for by the law comes knowledge of sin.
verses.kjv: Therefore by the deeds of the law there shall no flesh be justified in his sight: for by the law is the knowledge of sin.

words.gloss: I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify
before the face of, in the presence of

verses.greek: Νυνὶ δὲ χωρὶς νόμου δικαιοσύνη θεοῦ πεφανέρωται, μαρτυρουμένη ὑπὸ τοῦ νόμου καὶ τῶν προφητῶν,
verses.mistral: Now without the law, the righteousness of God is revealed, bearing witness through the law and the prophets.
verses.kjv: But now the righteousness of God without the law is manifested, being witnessed by the law and the prophets;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I make clear, visible, or manifest

verses.greek: δικαιοσύνη δὲ θεοῦ διὰ πίστεως Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ, εἰς ⸀πάντας τοὺς πιστεύοντας, οὐ γάρ ἐστιν διαστολή.
verses.mistral: Righteousness of God is through faith in Jesus Christ for all who believe; there is no distinction.
verses.kjv: Even the righteousness of God which is by faith of Jesus Christ unto all and upon all them that believe: for there is no difference:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: πάντες γὰρ ἥμαρτον καὶ ὑστεροῦνται τῆς δόξης τοῦ θεοῦ,
verses.mistral: All have sinned and fall short of the glory of God.
verses.kjv: For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God;

words.gloss: I am lacking, fall short, suffer need

verses.greek: δικαιούμενοι δωρεὰν τῇ αὐτοῦ χάριτι διὰ τῆς ἀπολυτρώσεως τῆς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ·
verses.mistral: Justified as a gift by His grace, through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:

words.gloss: I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify
as a free gift, without payment, freely

verses.greek: ὃν προέθετο ὁ θεὸς ἱλαστήριον ⸀διὰ πίστεως ἐν τῷ αὐτοῦ αἵματι εἰς ἔνδειξιν τῆς δικαιοσύνης αὐτοῦ διὰ τὴν πάρεσιν τῶν προγεγονότων ἁμαρτημάτων
verses.mistral: Justified in advance as a demonstration of God's righteousness through faith in his blood.
verses.kjv: Whom God hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood, to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God;

verses.greek: ἐν τῇ ἀνοχῇ τοῦ θεοῦ, πρὸς ⸀τὴν ἔνδειξιν τῆς δικαιοσύνης αὐτοῦ ἐν τῷ νῦν καιρῷ, εἰς τὸ εἶναι αὐτὸν δίκαιον καὶ δικαιοῦντα τὸν ἐκ πίστεως Ἰησοῦ.
verses.mistral: In the goodness of God, for the demonstration of His righteousness in the present time, to be righteous and justifying Him who is from faith in Jesus.
verses.kjv: To declare, I say, at this time his righteousness: that he might be just, and the justifier of him which believeth in Jesus.

words.gloss: I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify

verses.greek: Ποῦ οὖν ἡ καύχησις; ἐξεκλείσθη. διὰ ποίου νόμου; τῶν ἔργων; οὐχί, ἀλλὰ διὰ νόμου πίστεως.
verses.mistral: Where then is boasting? It is excluded. By what kind of law? Of works? Not at all, but by the law of faith.
verses.kjv: Where is boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? of works? Nay: but by the law of faith.

words.gloss: by no means, not at all

verses.greek: λογιζόμεθα ⸀γὰρ ⸂δικαιοῦσθαι πίστει⸃ ἄνθρωπον χωρὶς ἔργων νόμου.
verses.mistral: Justify a man without works of the law.
verses.kjv: Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law.

words.gloss: I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify

verses.greek: ἢ Ἰουδαίων ὁ θεὸς μόνον; ⸀οὐχὶ καὶ ἐθνῶν; ναὶ καὶ ἐθνῶν,
verses.mistral: Gentiles also
verses.kjv: Is he the God of the Jews only? is he not also of the Gentiles? Yes, of the Gentiles also:

words.gloss: by no means, not at all

verses.greek: ⸀εἴπερ εἷς ὁ θεός, ὃς δικαιώσει περιτομὴν ἐκ πίστεως καὶ ἀκροβυστίαν διὰ τῆς πίστεως.
verses.mistral: Justifies circumcision by faith, and uncircumcision through faith.
verses.kjv: Seeing it is one God, which shall justify the circumcision by faith, and uncircumcision through faith.

words.gloss: I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify

verses.greek: νόμον οὖν καταργοῦμεν διὰ τῆς πίστεως; μὴ γένοιτο, ἀλλὰ νόμον ⸀ἱστάνομεν.
verses.mistral: We establish the law, through faith; far from it, but we uphold the law.
verses.kjv: Do we then make void the law through faith? God forbid: yea, we establish the law.

words.gloss: I bring to naught, sever, abolish
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Τί οὖν ἐροῦμεν ⸀εὑρηκέναι Ἀβραὰμ τὸν ⸀προπάτορα ⸀ἡμῶν κατὰ σάρκα;
verses.mistral: We say that Abraham, our forefather, was found in faith.
verses.kjv: What shall we say then that Abraham our father, as pertaining to the flesh, hath found?

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: εἰ γὰρ Ἀβραὰμ ἐξ ἔργων ἐδικαιώθη, ἔχει καύχημα· ἀλλ’ οὐ ⸀πρὸς θεόν,
verses.mistral: If Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast; but not before God.
verses.kjv: For if Abraham were justified by works, he hath whereof to glory; but not before God.

words.gloss: I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify

verses.greek: τί γὰρ ἡ γραφὴ λέγει; Ἐπίστευσεν δὲ Ἀβραὰμ τῷ θεῷ καὶ ἐλογίσθη αὐτῷ εἰς δικαιοσύνην.
verses.mistral: What does the scripture say? Abraham believed God, and he was credited as righteousness.
verses.kjv: For what saith the scripture? Abraham believed God, and it was counted unto him for righteousness.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τῷ δὲ ἐργαζομένῳ ὁ μισθὸς οὐ λογίζεται κατὰ χάριν ἀλλὰ κατὰ ὀφείλημα·
verses.mistral: The one working is not credited for righteousness based on grace, but based on debt.
verses.kjv: Now to him that worketh is the reward not reckoned of grace, but of debt.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
down from, against, according to, throughout, during
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: τῷ δὲ μὴ ἐργαζομένῳ, πιστεύοντι δὲ ἐπὶ τὸν δικαιοῦντα τὸν ἀσεβῆ, λογίζεται ἡ πίστις αὐτοῦ εἰς δικαιοσύνην,
verses.mistral: Faith to the ungodly is credited for righteousness.
verses.kjv: But to him that worketh not, but believeth on him that justifieth the ungodly, his faith is counted for righteousness.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at
I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify

verses.greek: καθάπερ καὶ Δαυὶδ λέγει τὸν μακαρισμὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ᾧ ὁ θεὸς λογίζεται δικαιοσύνην χωρὶς ἔργων·
verses.mistral: Just as David also says in the Psalms, "The man to whom God imputes righteousness apart from works."
verses.kjv: Even as David also describeth the blessedness of the man, unto whom God imputeth righteousness without works,

verses.greek: Μακάριοι ὧν ἀφέθησαν αἱ ἀνομίαι καὶ ὧν ἐπεκαλύφθησαν αἱ ἁμαρτίαι,
verses.mistral: Blessed are the ones whose lawless acts are forgiven, and whose sins are covered.
verses.kjv: Saying, Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven, and whose sins are covered.

words.gloss: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.greek: μακάριος ἀνὴρ ⸀οὗ οὐ μὴ λογίσηται κύριος ἁμαρτίαν.
verses.mistral: Blessed is the man to whom the Lord imputes not iniquity.
verses.kjv: Blessed is the man to whom the Lord will not impute sin.

verses.greek: Ὁ μακαρισμὸς οὖν οὗτος ἐπὶ τὴν περιτομὴν ἢ καὶ ἐπὶ τὴν ἀκροβυστίαν; λέγομεν ⸀γάρ· Ἐλογίσθη τῷ Ἀβραὰμ ἡ πίστις εἰς δικαιοσύνην.
verses.mistral: This man is blessed whether by circumcision or uncircumcision. We say, indeed, that faith was credited to Abraham for righteousness.
verses.kjv: Cometh this blessedness then upon the circumcision only, or upon the uncircumcision also? for we say that faith was reckoned to Abraham for righteousness.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: πῶς οὖν ἐλογίσθη; ἐν περιτομῇ ὄντι ἢ ἐν ἀκροβυστίᾳ; οὐκ ἐν περιτομῇ ἀλλ’ ἐν ἀκροβυστίᾳ·
verses.mistral: How then was it credited? In circumcision or uncircumcision? Not in circumcision but in uncircumcision.
verses.kjv: How was it then reckoned? when he was in circumcision, or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision.

verses.greek: καὶ σημεῖον ἔλαβεν περιτομῆς, σφραγῖδα τῆς δικαιοσύνης τῆς πίστεως τῆς ἐν τῇ ἀκροβυστίᾳ, εἰς τὸ εἶναι αὐτὸν πατέρα πάντων τῶν πιστευόντων δι’ ἀκροβυστίας, εἰς τὸ ⸀λογισθῆναι αὐτοῖς τὴν δικαιοσύνην,
verses.mistral: Sign of circumcision was received, seal of the righteousness of faith in uncircumcision, for him to be father of all those believing through uncircumcision, for them to be accounted righteous.
verses.kjv: And he received the sign of circumcision, a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had yet being uncircumcised: that he might be the father of all them that believe, though they be not circumcised; that righteousness might be imputed unto them also:

verses.greek: καὶ πατέρα περιτομῆς τοῖς οὐκ ἐκ περιτομῆς μόνον ἀλλὰ καὶ τοῖς στοιχοῦσιν τοῖς ἴχνεσιν τῆς ⸂ἐν ἀκροβυστίᾳ πίστεως⸃ τοῦ πατρὸς ἡμῶν Ἀβραάμ.
verses.mistral: And the father of circumcision to those not from circumcision, also to those who are the heirs of the promise, that is, the faith of our father Abraham.
verses.kjv: And the father of circumcision to them who are not of the circumcision only, but who also walk in the steps of that faith of our father Abraham, which he had being yet uncircumcised.

verses.greek: Οὐ γὰρ διὰ νόμου ἡ ἐπαγγελία τῷ Ἀβραὰμ ἢ τῷ σπέρματι αὐτοῦ, τὸ κληρονόμον αὐτὸν ⸀εἶναι κόσμου, ἀλλὰ διὰ δικαιοσύνης πίστεως·
verses.mistral: Through faith, not through the law, was the promise to Abraham or to his seed, that he should be the heir of the world, but through righteousness of faith.
verses.kjv: For the promise, that he should be the heir of the world, was not to Abraham, or to his seed, through the law, but through the righteousness of faith.

verses.greek: εἰ γὰρ οἱ ἐκ νόμου κληρονόμοι, κεκένωται ἡ πίστις καὶ κατήργηται ἡ ἐπαγγελία·

verses.mistral: Human, I am saying this because of the weakness of your flesh. Just as you presented your members as slaves to impurity and wickedness leading to more wickedness, so now present your members as slaves to righteousness leading to holiness.
verses.kjv: I speak after the manner of men because of the infirmity of your flesh: for as ye have yielded your members servants to uncleanness and to iniquity unto iniquity; even so now yield your members servants to righteousness unto holiness.

verses.kjv: But now we are delivered from the law, that being dead wherein we were held; that we should serve in newness of spirit, and not in the oldness of the letter.

verses.kjv: For rulers are not a terror to good works, but to the evil. Wilt thou then not be afraid of the power? do that which is good, and thou shalt have praise of the same:

verses.kjv: And that, knowing the time, that now it is high time to awake out of sleep: for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed.

verses.greek: ἡ νὺξ προέκοψεν, ἡ δὲ ἡμέρα ἤγγικεν. ⸀ἀποβαλώμεθα οὖν τὰ ἔργα τοῦ σκότους, ⸂ἐνδυσώμεθα δὲ⸃ τὰ ὅπλα τοῦ φωτός.
verses.mistral: The night is past; the day has come. Let us therefore put off the works of darkness, and put on the armor of light.
verses.kjv: The night is far spent, the day is at hand: let us therefore cast off the works of darkness, and let us put on the armour of light.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I throw away from, throw overboard
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὡς ἐν ἡμέρᾳ εὐσχημόνως περιπατήσωμεν, μὴ κώμοις καὶ μέθαις, μὴ κοίταις καὶ ἀσελγείαις, μὴ ἔριδι καὶ ζήλῳ,
verses.mistral: Walk properly as in the day, not in unwise parties, not in drunkenness, not in sexual immorality and sensuality, not in quarrels and jealousy.
verses.kjv: Let us walk honestly, as in the day; not in rioting and drunkenness, not in chambering and wantonness, not in strife and envying.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ ἐνδύσασθε τὸν κύριον Ἰησοῦν Χριστόν, καὶ τῆς σαρκὸς πρόνοιαν μὴ ποιεῖσθε εἰς ἐπιθυμίας.
verses.mistral: Put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and do not present your members as instruments of unrighteousness.
verses.kjv: But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof.

verses.greek: Τὸν δὲ ἀσθενοῦντα τῇ πίστει προσλαμβάνεσθε, μὴ εἰς διακρίσεις διαλογισμῶν.
verses.mistral: Strengthen the weak, being of faith, not for judgments by words.
verses.kjv: Him that is weak in the faith receive ye, but not to doubtful disputations.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὃς μὲν πιστεύει φαγεῖν πάντα, ὁ δὲ ἀσθενῶν λάχανα ἐσθίει.
verses.mistral: He who believes is to eat all things, but he who has weak faith eats vegetables.
verses.kjv: For one believeth that he may eat all things: another, who is weak, eateth herbs.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ ἐσθίων τὸν μὴ ἐσθίοντα μὴ ἐξουθενείτω, ⸂ὁ δὲ⸃ μὴ ἐσθίων τὸν ἐσθίοντα μὴ κρινέτω, ὁ θεὸς γὰρ αὐτὸν προσελάβετο.
verses.mistral: Let him who eats not judge him who does eat, for God has welcomed him.
verses.kjv: Let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not; and let not him which eateth not judge him that eateth: for God hath received him.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: σὺ τίς εἶ ὁ κρίνων ἀλλότριον οἰκέτην; τῷ ἰδίῳ κυρίῳ στήκει ἢ πίπτει· σταθήσεται δέ, ⸂δυνατεῖ γὰρ⸃ ὁ ⸀κύριος στῆσαι αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: You who are judging another's servant: does it matter to you if your own lord stands or falls? Indeed, the Lord is able to maintain him.
verses.kjv: Who art thou that judgest another man's servant? to his own master he standeth or falleth. Yea, he shall be holden up: for God is able to make him stand.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ὃς ⸀μὲν κρίνει ἡμέραν παρ’ ἡμέραν, ὃς δὲ κρίνει πᾶσαν ἡμέραν· ἕκαστος ἐν τῷ ἰδίῳ νοῒ πληροφορείσθω·
verses.mistral: Whoever judges a day from another day, and whoever judges every day, let each person in his own conscience be established.
verses.kjv: One man esteemeth one day above another: another esteemeth every day alike. Let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I carry out fully, fully convince

verses.greek: ὁ φρονῶν τὴν ἡμέραν κυρίῳ ⸀φρονεῖ. καὶ ὁ ἐσθίων κυρίῳ ἐσθίει, εὐχαριστεῖ γὰρ τῷ θεῷ· καὶ ὁ μὴ ἐσθίων κυρίῳ οὐκ ἐσθίει, καὶ εὐχαριστεῖ τῷ θεῷ.
verses.mistral: The one thinking the day to the Lord thinks rightly. The one eating the Lord eats, for he gives thanks to God. The one not eating to the Lord does not eat, and still gives thanks to God.
verses.kjv: He that regardeth the day, regardeth it unto the Lord; and he that regardeth not the day, to the Lord he doth not regard it. He that eateth, eateth to the Lord, for he giveth God thanks; and he that eateth not, to the Lord he eateth not, and giveth God thanks.

verses.greek: Οὐδεὶς γὰρ ἡμῶν ἑαυτῷ ζῇ, καὶ οὐδεὶς ἑαυτῷ ἀποθνῄσκει·
verses.mistral: None of us lives to himself, and none dies to himself.
verses.kjv: For none of us liveth to himself, and no man dieth to himself.

words.gloss: I am dying, am about to die

verses.greek: ἐάν τε γὰρ ζῶμεν, τῷ κυρίῳ ζῶμεν, ἐάν τε ἀποθνῄσκωμεν, τῷ κυρίῳ ἀποθνῄσκομεν. ἐάν τε οὖν ζῶμεν ἐάν τε ἀποθνῄσκωμεν, τοῦ κυρίου ἐσμέν.
verses.mistral: If we live, we live to the Lord, if we die, we die to the Lord. Whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord.
verses.kjv: For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we die unto the Lord: whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord's.

words.gloss: I am dying, am about to die
I am dying, am about to die
I am dying, am about to die

verses.greek: εἰς τοῦτο γὰρ ⸀Χριστὸς ⸀ἀπέθανεν καὶ ἔζησεν ἵνα καὶ νεκρῶν καὶ ζώντων κυριεύσῃ.
verses.mistral: For this reason Christ died and lived again, so that He may also reign as Lord over the dead and the living.
verses.kjv: For to this end Christ both died, and rose, and revived, that he might be Lord both of the dead and living.

words.gloss: I am dying, am about to die

verses.greek: Σὺ δὲ τί κρίνεις τὸν ἀδελφόν σου; ἢ καὶ σὺ τί ἐξουθενεῖς τὸν ἀδελφόν σου; πάντες γὰρ παραστησόμεθα τῷ βήματι τοῦ ⸀θεοῦ,
verses.mistral: You judge your brother? Or do you also despise your brother? All shall stand before the judgment seat of God.
verses.kjv: But why dost thou judge thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother? for we shall all stand before the judgment seat of Christ.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I bring, present, come up to and stand by

verses.greek: γέγραπται γάρ· Ζῶ ἐγώ, λέγει κύριος, ὅτι ἐμοὶ κάμψει πᾶν γόνυ, καὶ ⸂πᾶσα γλῶσσα ἐξομολογήσεται⸃ τῷ θεῷ.
verses.mistral: Writes the Lord, I will bring every knee to me, and every tongue shall confess to God.
verses.kjv: For it is written, As I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God.

verses.greek: ⸀ἄρα ἕκαστος ἡμῶν περὶ ἑαυτοῦ λόγον ⸀δώσει.
verses.mistral: Each of us will give an account for himself.
verses.kjv: So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.

words.gloss: a particle asking a question, expecting a negative response

verses.greek: Μηκέτι οὖν ἀλλήλους κρίνωμεν· ἀλλὰ τοῦτο κρίνατε μᾶλλον, τὸ μὴ τιθέναι πρόσκομμα τῷ ἀδελφῷ ἢ σκάνδαλον.
verses.mistral: Judge not one another. But rather judge this: to put a stumbling block before the brother, or an occasion of sin.
verses.kjv: Let us not therefore judge one another any more: but judge this rather, that no man put a stumblingblock or an occasion to fall in his brother's way.

verses.greek: οἶδα καὶ πέπεισμαι ἐν κυρίῳ Ἰησοῦ ὅτι οὐδὲν κοινὸν δι’ ⸀ἑαυτοῦ· εἰ μὴ τῷ λογιζομένῳ τι κοινὸν εἶναι, ἐκείνῳ κοινόν.
verses.mistral: I know and am convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself. However, to the one who thinks something is unclean, it is unclean for them.
verses.kjv: I know, and am persuaded by the Lord Jesus, that there is nothing unclean of itself: but to him that esteemeth any thing to be unclean, to him it is unclean.

verses.greek: εἰ ⸀γὰρ διὰ βρῶμα ὁ ἀδελφός σου λυπεῖται, οὐκέτι κατὰ ἀγάπην περιπατεῖς. μὴ τῷ βρώματί σου ἐκεῖνον ἀπόλλυε ὑπὲρ οὗ Χριστὸς ἀπέθανεν.
verses.mistral: If your brother is grieved by food, you are no longer walking in love. Do not let food cause your brother to stumble for whom Christ died.
verses.kjv: But if thy brother be grieved with thy meat, now walkest thou not charitably. Destroy not him with thy meat, for whom Christ died.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
I am dying, am about to die

verses.greek: μὴ βλασφημείσθω οὖν ὑμῶν τὸ ἀγαθόν.
verses.mistral: Let not your good be slandered.
verses.kjv: Let not then your good be evil spoken of:

verses.greek: οὐ γάρ ἐστιν ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ βρῶσις καὶ πόσις, ἀλλὰ δικαιοσύνη καὶ εἰρήνη καὶ χαρὰ ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ·
verses.mistral: The kingdom of God is not food and drink, but righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: For the kingdom of God is not meat and drink; but righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.

verses.greek: ὁ γὰρ ἐν ⸀τούτῳ δουλεύων τῷ Χριστῷ εὐάρεστος τῷ θεῷ καὶ δόκιμος τοῖς ἀνθρώποις.
verses.mistral: Serving in this way, Christ is acceptable to God and approved by men.
verses.kjv: For he that in these things serveth Christ is acceptable to God, and approved of men.

words.gloss: I am a slave, am subject to, obey

verses.greek: ἄρα οὖν τὰ τῆς εἰρήνης διώκωμεν καὶ τὰ τῆς οἰκοδομῆς τῆς εἰς ἀλλήλους.
verses.mistral: Pursue peace and pursue the building up of one another.
verses.kjv: Let us therefore follow after the things which make for peace, and things wherewith one may edify another.

words.gloss: a particle asking a question, expecting a negative response

verses.greek: μὴ ἕνεκεν βρώματος κατάλυε τὸ ἔργον τοῦ θεοῦ. πάντα μὲν καθαρά, ἀλλὰ κακὸν τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ τῷ διὰ προσκόμματος ἐσθίοντι.
verses.mistral: Obstruct not the work of God for the sake of food. All things are clean, but to the one who eats with an eye toward self-indulgence, it is unclean.
verses.kjv: For meat destroy not the work of God. All things indeed are pure; but it is evil for that man who eateth with offence.

words.gloss: for the sake of, on account of, wherefore
I break up, overthrow, unharness, find a lodging

verses.greek: καλὸν τὸ μὴ φαγεῖν κρέα μηδὲ πιεῖν οἶνον μηδὲ ἐν ᾧ ὁ ἀδελφός σου προσκόπτει ⸂ἢ σκανδαλίζεται ἢ ἀσθενεῖ⸃·
verses.mistral: It is good not to eat meat or drink wine, nor be caused to stumble by your brother.
verses.kjv: It is good neither to eat flesh, nor to drink wine, nor any thing whereby thy brother stumbleth, or is offended, or is made weak.

verses.greek: σὺ πίστιν ⸀ἣν ἔχεις κατὰ σεαυτὸν ἔχε ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ. μακάριος ὁ μὴ κρίνων ἑαυτὸν ἐν ᾧ δοκιμάζει·
verses.mistral: Blessed is the one who does not condemn himself in what he approves.
verses.kjv: Hast thou faith? have it to thyself before God. Happy is he that condemneth not himself in that thing which he alloweth.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
before the face of, in the presence of
I put to the test, prove, examine

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ διακρινόμενος ἐὰν φάγῃ κατακέκριται, ὅτι οὐκ ἐκ πίστεως· πᾶν δὲ ὃ οὐκ ἐκ πίστεως ἁμαρτία ⸀ἐστίν.
verses.mistral: The condemned person, if he eats, is already judged, because he has not from faith; for whatever is not from faith is sin.
verses.kjv: And he that doubteth is damned if he eat, because he eateth not of faith: for whatsoever is not of faith is sin.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ὀφείλομεν δὲ ἡμεῖς οἱ δυνατοὶ τὰ ἀσθενήματα τῶν ἀδυνάτων βαστάζειν, καὶ μὴ ἑαυτοῖς ἀρέσκειν.
verses.mistral: We ought to carry the weaknesses of those who are weak, and not please ourselves.
verses.kjv: We then that are strong ought to bear the infirmities of the weak, and not to please ourselves.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἕκαστος ἡμῶν τῷ πλησίον ἀρεσκέτω εἰς τὸ ἀγαθὸν πρὸς οἰκοδομήν·
verses.mistral: Each of us should please our neighbor for good, toward edification.
verses.kjv: Let every one of us please his neighbour for his good to edification.

verses.greek: καὶ γὰρ ὁ Χριστὸς οὐχ ἑαυτῷ ἤρεσεν· ἀλλὰ καθὼς γέγραπται· Οἱ ὀνειδισμοὶ τῶν ὀνειδιζόντων σε ἐπέπεσαν ἐπ’ ἐμέ.
verses.mistral: And for Christ did not please Himself, but just as it is written: "The insults of those who insult you have fallen on me."
verses.kjv: For even Christ pleased not himself; but, as it is written, The reproaches of them that reproached thee fell on me.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ὅσα γὰρ προεγράφη, ⸀εἰς τὴν ἡμετέραν διδασκαλίαν ⸀ἐγράφη, ἵνα διὰ τῆς ὑπομονῆς καὶ διὰ τῆς παρακλήσεως τῶν γραφῶν τὴν ἐλπίδα ἔχωμεν.
verses.mistral: The things which were written beforehand, have been committed to us for our learning, through the patience and comfort of the Scriptures, we may have hope.
verses.kjv: For whatsoever things were written aforetime were written for our learning, that we through patience and comfort of the scriptures might have hope.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ θεὸς τῆς ὑπομονῆς καὶ τῆς παρακλήσεως δῴη ὑμῖν τὸ αὐτὸ φρονεῖν ἐν ἀλλήλοις κατὰ ⸂Χριστὸν Ἰησοῦν⸃,
verses.mistral: The God of endurance and encouragement grant you to think the same way, one toward another, according to Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: Now the God of patience and consolation grant you to be likeminded one toward another according to Christ Jesus:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ἵνα ὁμοθυμαδὸν ἐν ἑνὶ στόματι δοξάζητε τὸν θεὸν καὶ πατέρα τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: In one accord, exalt as God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: That ye may with one mind and one mouth glorify God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

words.gloss: I glorify, honor, bestow glory on

verses.greek: Διὸ προσλαμβάνεσθε ἀλλήλους, καθὼς καὶ ὁ Χριστὸς προσελάβετο ⸀ὑμᾶς, εἰς δόξαν ⸀τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: Therefore, accept one another, just as Christ also accepted you, to the glory of God.
verses.kjv: Wherefore receive ye one another, as Christ also received us to the glory of God.

verses.greek: λέγω ⸀γὰρ ⸀Χριστὸν διάκονον ⸀γεγενῆσθαι περιτομῆς ὑπὲρ ἀληθείας θεοῦ, εἰς τὸ βεβαιῶσαι τὰς ἐπαγγελίας τῶν πατέρων,
verses.mistral: Christ became a servant of the circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises of the fathers.
verses.kjv: Now I say that Jesus Christ was a minister of the circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises made unto the fathers:

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: τὰ δὲ ἔθνη ὑπὲρ ἐλέους δοξάσαι τὸν θεόν· καθὼς γέγραπται· Διὰ τοῦτο ἐξομολογήσομαί σοι ἐν ἔθνεσι, καὶ τῷ ὀνόματί σου ψαλῶ.
verses.mistral: Nations will praise You for Your mercy; As it is written, "Because of this I will confess You among the nations, And sing praises to Your name."
verses.kjv: And that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy; as it is written, For this cause I will confess to thee among the Gentiles, and sing unto thy name.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I glorify, honor, bestow glory on

verses.greek: καὶ πάλιν λέγει· Εὐφράνθητε, ἔθνη, μετὰ τοῦ λαοῦ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Rejoice together with the people of God, nations.
verses.kjv: And again he saith, Rejoice, ye Gentiles, with his people.

words.gloss: again, further, on the other hand
I cheer, am glad, make merry

verses.greek: καὶ πάλιν· Αἰνεῖτε, ⸂πάντα τὰ ἔθνη, τὸν κύριον⸃, καὶ ⸀ἐπαινεσάτωσαν αὐτὸν πάντες οἱ λαοί.
verses.mistral: Praise the Lord, all nations, and let all people extol him.
verses.kjv: And again, Praise the Lord, all ye Gentiles; and laud him, all ye people.

words.gloss: again, further, on the other hand

verses.greek: καὶ πάλιν Ἠσαΐας λέγει· Ἔσται ἡ ῥίζα τοῦ Ἰεσσαί, καὶ ὁ ἀνιστάμενος ἄρχειν ἐθνῶν· ἐπ’ αὐτῷ ἔθνη ἐλπιοῦσιν.
verses.mistral: And again Isaiah says, "The root of Jesse shall stand as a signal for the peoples; on him the nations will hope."
verses.kjv: And again, Esaias saith, There shall be a root of Jesse, and he that shall rise to reign over the Gentiles; in him shall the Gentiles trust.

words.gloss: again, further, on the other hand
I raise up, set up, rise from
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ θεὸς τῆς ἐλπίδος πληρώσαι ὑμᾶς πάσης χαρᾶς καὶ εἰρήνης ἐν τῷ πιστεύειν, εἰς τὸ περισσεύειν ὑμᾶς ἐν τῇ ἐλπίδι ἐν δυνάμει πνεύματος ἁγίου.
verses.mistral: God will fulfill in you all joy and peace through faith, that you may abound in hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that ye may abound in hope, through the power of the Holy Ghost.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Πέπεισμαι δέ, ἀδελφοί μου, καὶ αὐτὸς ἐγὼ περὶ ὑμῶν, ὅτι καὶ αὐτοὶ μεστοί ἐστε ἀγαθωσύνης, πεπληρωμένοι ⸀πάσης γνώσεως, δυνάμενοι καὶ ⸀ἀλλήλους νουθετεῖν.
verses.mistral: I am persuaded, brethren, concerning you, that you also are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish one another.
verses.kjv: And I myself also am persuaded of you, my brethren, that ye also are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish one another.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸀τολμηρότερον δὲ ἔγραψα ⸀ὑμῖν ἀπὸ μέρους, ὡς ἐπαναμιμνῄσκων ὑμᾶς, διὰ τὴν χάριν τὴν δοθεῖσάν μοι ⸀ὑπὸ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.mistral: More boldly I write to you, as though I were making an appeal, through the grace given to me by God
verses.kjv: Nevertheless, brethren, I have written the more boldly unto you in some sort, as putting you in mind, because of the grace that is given to me of God,

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: εἰς τὸ εἶναί με λειτουργὸν ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃ εἰς τὰ ἔθνη, ἱερουργοῦντα τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τοῦ θεοῦ, ἵνα γένηται ἡ προσφορὰ τῶν ἐθνῶν εὐπρόσδεκτος, ἡγιασμένη ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ.
verses.mistral: Serving as a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, ministering the gospel of God, so that the offering of the Gentiles may be acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: That I should be the minister of Jesus Christ to the Gentiles, ministering the gospel of God, that the offering up of the Gentiles might be acceptable, being sanctified by the Holy Ghost.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ἔχω οὖν ⸀τὴν καύχησιν ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ τὰ πρὸς τὸν θεόν·
verses.mistral: I have the boast in Christ Jesus concerning God.
verses.kjv: I have therefore whereof I may glory through Jesus Christ in those things which pertain to God.

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ τολμήσω ⸂τι λαλεῖν⸃ ὧν οὐ κατειργάσατο Χριστὸς δι’ ἐμοῦ εἰς ὑπακοὴν ἐθνῶν, λόγῳ καὶ ἔργῳ,
verses.mistral: I will not boast anything that has not been accomplished by Christ through me in obedience to the Gentiles, in word and deed.
verses.kjv: For I will not dare to speak of any of those things which Christ hath not wrought by me, to make the Gentiles obedient, by word and deed,

verses.greek: ἐν δυνάμει σημείων καὶ τεράτων, ἐν δυνάμει ⸀πνεύματος· ὥστε με ἀπὸ Ἰερουσαλὴμ καὶ κύκλῳ μέχρι τοῦ Ἰλλυρικοῦ πεπληρωκέναι τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τοῦ Χριστοῦ,
verses.mistral: In power of signs and wonders, in the power of the Spirit, so as to complete the gospel of Christ from Jerusalem round about to Illyricum.
verses.kjv: Through mighty signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God; so that from Jerusalem, and round about unto Illyricum, I have fully preached the gospel of Christ.

verses.greek: οὕτως δὲ ⸀φιλοτιμούμενον εὐαγγελίζεσθαι οὐχ ὅπου ὠνομάσθη Χριστός, ἵνα μὴ ἐπ’ ἀλλότριον θεμέλιον οἰκοδομῶ,
verses.mistral: Therefore I am eager to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
verses.kjv: Yea, so have I strived to preach the gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build upon another man's foundation:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I bring good news, preach good tidings
I give a name to, mention
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ καθὼς γέγραπται· ⸂Οἷς οὐκ ἀνηγγέλη περὶ αὐτοῦ ὄψονται⸃, καὶ οἳ οὐκ ἀκηκόασιν συνήσουσιν.
verses.mistral: Those who have not heard will see, and those who have not heeded will understand.
verses.kjv: But as it is written, To whom he was not spoken of, they shall see: and they that have not heard shall understand.

verses.greek: Διὸ καὶ ἐνεκοπτόμην τὰ πολλὰ τοῦ ἐλθεῖν πρὸς ὑμᾶς·
verses.mistral: Therefore, I have been often hindered from coming to you
verses.kjv: For which cause also I have been much hindered from coming to you.

verses.greek: νυνὶ δὲ μηκέτι τόπον ἔχων ἐν τοῖς κλίμασι τούτοις, ἐπιποθίαν δὲ ἔχων τοῦ ἐλθεῖν πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἀπὸ ⸀ἱκανῶν ἐτῶν,
verses.mistral: Now lacking a place in these climates, yet eager to come to you, after many years.
verses.kjv: But now having no more place in these parts, and having a great desire these many years to come unto you;

verses.greek: ὡς ⸀ἂν πορεύωμαι εἰς τὴν ⸀Σπανίαν, ἐλπίζω γὰρ διαπορευόμενος θεάσασθαι ὑμᾶς καὶ ὑφ’ ὑμῶν προπεμφθῆναι ἐκεῖ ἐὰν ὑμῶν πρῶτον ἀπὸ μέρους ἐμπλησθῶ—
verses.mistral: I hope to visit you in Spain, and to be assisted by you there, if at all possible among you.
verses.kjv: Whensoever I take my journey into Spain, I will come to you: for I trust to see you in my journey, and to be brought on my way thitherward by you, if first I be somewhat filled with your company.

verses.greek: νυνὶ δὲ πορεύομαι εἰς Ἰερουσαλὴμ διακονῶν τοῖς ἁγίοις.
verses.mistral: I now go to Jerusalem, serving the holy ones.
verses.kjv: But now I go unto Jerusalem to minister unto the saints.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: εὐδόκησαν γὰρ Μακεδονία καὶ Ἀχαΐα κοινωνίαν τινὰ ποιήσασθαι εἰς τοὺς πτωχοὺς τῶν ἁγίων τῶν ἐν Ἰερουσαλήμ.
verses.mistral: Approved it is, Macedonia and Achaia share fellowship in contributing to the poor among the saints in Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: For it hath pleased them of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor saints which are at Jerusalem.

verses.greek: εὐδόκησαν γάρ, καὶ ὀφειλέται ⸂εἰσὶν αὐτῶν⸃· εἰ γὰρ τοῖς πνευματικοῖς αὐτῶν ἐκοινώνησαν τὰ ἔθνη, ὀφείλουσιν καὶ ἐν τοῖς σαρκικοῖς λειτουργῆσαι αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: Obligated they are, and it is fitting for them — if indeed they have shared with the spiritual ones among them, then they also ought to minister to them in their carnal matters.
verses.kjv: It hath pleased them verily; and their debtors they are. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things, their duty is also to minister unto them in carnal things.

words.gloss: I share, communicate, have fellowship with

verses.greek: τοῦτο οὖν ἐπιτελέσας, καὶ σφραγισάμενος αὐτοῖς τὸν καρπὸν τοῦτον, ἀπελεύσομαι δι’ ὑμῶν ⸀εἰς Σπανίαν·
verses.mistral: Therefore, after I have completed this and sealed this fruit among you, I will depart for Spain.
verses.kjv: When therefore I have performed this, and have sealed to them this fruit, I will come by you into Spain.

words.gloss: I come or go away from, depart, return

verses.greek: οἶδα δὲ ὅτι ἐρχόμενος πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐν πληρώματι ⸀εὐλογίας Χριστοῦ ἐλεύσομαι.
verses.mistral: Coming to you in the fullness of Christ's blessings.
verses.kjv: And I am sure that, when I come unto you, I shall come in the fulness of the blessing of the gospel of Christ.

verses.mistral: I implore you, brethren, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to strive for me in prayers to God.
verses.kjv: Now I beseech you, brethren, for the Lord Jesus Christ's sake, and for the love of the Spirit, that ye strive together with me in your prayers to God for me;

verses.mistral: That I might be delivered from those disobedient in Judea, and my service which is to Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints
verses.kjv: That I may be delivered from them that do not believe in Judaea; and that my service which I have for Jerusalem may be accepted of the saints;

verses.mistral: In the joy, having come to you, I will rest with you according to God's will.
verses.kjv: That I may come unto you with joy by the will of God, and may with you be refreshed.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ θεὸς τῆς εἰρήνης μετὰ πάντων ὑμῶν· ἀμήν.
verses.mistral: The God of peace be with you all. Amen.

verses.mistral: Present to you Phoebe, our sister, also a servant of the church in Cenchrea.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.mistral: Receive Phoebe in the Lord, worthy of the saints, and aid her in anything you may have need of; for she has been a patron of many, also of me.
verses.kjv: That ye receive her in the Lord, as becometh saints, and that ye assist her in whatsoever business she hath need of you: for she hath been a succourer of many, and of myself also.

verses.kjv: Greet Priscilla and Aquila my helpers in Christ Jesus:

words.gloss: I greet, salute, pay my respects to

verses.mistral: Those who risked their own necks for my life, to them I am not only grateful, but all the churches of the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: Who have for my life laid down their own necks: unto whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.

verses.greek: καὶ τὴν κατ’ οἶκον αὐτῶν ἐκκλησίαν. ἀσπάσασθε Ἐπαίνετον τὸν ἀγαπητόν μου, ὅς ἐστιν ἀπαρχὴ τῆς ⸀Ἀσίας εἰς Χριστόν.

verses.kjv: Likewise greet the church that is in their house. Salute my wellbeloved Epaenetus, who is the firstfruits of Achaia unto Christ.

verses.kjv: Greet Mary, who bestowed much labour on us.

verses.kjv: Salute Philologus, and Julia, Nereus, and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints which are with them.

verses.mistral: Greet one another with a holy kiss. The churches greet you all in Christ.
verses.kjv: Salute one another with an holy kiss. The churches of Christ salute you.

verses.kjv: Now I beseech you, brethren, mark them which cause divisions and offences contrary to the doctrine which ye have learned; and avoid them.

verses.mistral: These do not serve our Lord Christ, but their own belly; and by fair and flattering speech deceive the hearts of the naive.
verses.kjv: For they that are such serve not our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by good words and fair speeches deceive the hearts of the simple.

verses.mistral: Your obedience has become known to all; therefore I rejoice over you. But I want you to be wise as to what is good, and simple concerning evil.
verses.kjv: For your obedience is come abroad unto all men. I am glad therefore on your behalf: but yet I would have you wise unto that which is good, and simple concerning evil.

verses.mistral: The God of peace will crush Satan under your feet shortly. Grace to you from our Lord Jesus Christ.
